query: My eww revealer never opens even though my variable is true. I have (revealer :reveal "${show_panel}") and a button that toggles show_panel. The reveal seems stuck. What's wrong?
expected_behavior: Identifies the wall: wrapping an expression in double quotes inside an attribute turns the result into a string literal, so :reveal receives the string "true"/"false" instead of a boolean
Routes to the ${} vs {} syntax rule (SKILL.md 'Syntax Rules' / 'Common Mistakes', or OPERATORS.md context)
Gives the fix: use a bare expression with {} and NO surrounding quotes — (revealer :reveal {show_panel})
Explains that ${} string interpolation is only for inside "..." or backtick strings, not for boolean/number attribute values
notes: Guards against the single most common eww expression error: quoting a boolean/number attribute value. (button :active "${count > 0}") or (revealer :reveal "${x}") yields a literal string 'true'/'false', which GTK does not treat as the boolean — the widget silently misbehaves. A naive answer blames the variable or the toggle logic instead of the quotes.
